transcript.text: How's it going?
Thank you, finally.
You too.
How's it going?
Good.
It is going.
It's been a busy day of back-to-back meetings.
You know how that is.
How long have you been at Rapyd?
I think I'm coming up on three years.
Time here really flies.
It feels like I just joined yesterday.
I think I'm almost at three years.
It's been...
Oh, my gosh.
I thought you were like a recent addition.
my gosh, no.
Not at all.
No, it's just I think now we're starting to really integrate social into more pieces of things.
It started off where social was in its own little universe, and we're getting better at making sure social is involved more.
That's awesome.
Also, hey, Nicolette, how are you?
Good.
How was Lisbon?
Nicolette, I hope you're feeling better.
Oh, my gosh.
I got wrecked.
But I don't know if it was a little bit or just in general, just getting sick.
I mean, international travel and then also COVID, kind of the worst of both worlds.
but was it fun?
It was so much fun.
Yeah, it was gorgeous.
Being with the team face-to-face was really nice, too.
I feel like we only see each other in these little boxes.
Yeah, it was the first time Ari and I met in person, so that was fun.
Hey, Mark, how's it going?
Good, how are you?
Awesome, good.
Yeah, I didn't realize that Ari has been at Rapyd for like years.
I was like, how long have you been with the company?
She's a veteran.
Surprise.
I'm so glad we could all get together and chat.
I know originally, I think the original framework for the conversation was just socials is becoming increasingly important, Ari is kind of running that, would be good to kind of just sync up and just
Discuss like what the general framework is for posting currently and then how we could leverage some of GrowthX's content and then like super brief background.
I joined GrowthX probably like six months or so ago, which again, it feels like six years, but it's been six months.

Worked as a journalist for about six and a half years at Business Insider covering like Tachycom partnerships, then moved into content marketing, worked at, a boutique agency that did mostly like interview-based long form and we also ran socials for a few of our clients and that was mostly like venture-backed B2B and so we ran socials for the founders typically and then also sometimes for the company account and we had kind of like a general formula that we rinsed and repeated, which worked pretty well.
And our kind of like bread and butter was really social proof.
So that was sort of the like most atomic level of the
The social posting strategy, but anyway, that's like my brief background, but defer to you, but just in case there's anything interesting or helpful, always available to chat.
Open to however we want to do this, but I would love to hear just like, how are you approaching socials currently?
And that'll help me figure out maybe how we can slot in and how best to organize, just like whether it's labels and Airtable or we can share, you know.
Yeah, so we're kind of in the process of reworking how we work as a team in general.
I think typically in the past, what's happened is social has kind of worked in its own silo where I pull content off of the blog based on what I've seen as like most recently published.
Or if there's any like white papers or reports that came out recently, I'll kind of use that to map out the social content calendar.
And then there's other pieces of content, which are.
For more campaign launches, or if there's like events and stuff like that, those are usually my driving forces.
And then the evergreen content is what gets slated in in between all of those gaps.
But there hasn't been a ton of, I guess, like integration between social and the content team.

And that's something we're kind of figuring out right now is like, how can we start creating more pieces of content that we can use, both from like the brand accounts for Rapyd and then the other side of things is sales enablement.
So we're doing a lot of social selling training for our sales teams.
And we actually just purchased a new tool called Octopost, which is going to help them be more active on social media.
So a big project that I'm working on with the content team is creating like a stream of content that we can constantly upload into Octopost.
So that our sales team has fresh content for them to publish on their LinkedIn.
So it's going to be a really great way to amplify a lot of the great work that everybody's already doing here.
It's just now we have more means and methods to get it out there, whether it's on the brand account or with our sales team helping amplify it.

I think we're still in the process of figuring out, and Mark and Nicolette, I think our next call is going to cover some of it, what that looks like in terms of aligning on a calendar that social content is accounted for that works with the new calendar that the content team's working on.
But I think in terms of growthx, from my understanding, you guys are supporting more SEO content, is my understanding.
don't know, Mark, if maybe you have more details to share.
But that's correct.
That's the primary focus of what we're doing is SEO and GEO.
Mm hmm.
Yeah.
So for that type of content, I don't know necessarily if it's the.
Type of blog posts we would want to promote on social.
Maybe there are a few if they're like high point.
But if they're just like more long form content that's focused on like ranking higher in search engines or through like AI, sometimes those aren't the best pieces of content to promote on social.
But we can still, you know, look at it and see if it makes sense.
Yeah, that totally makes sense.

And totally aligned there with anything that's too like top of funnel, general, just kind of the bet is slightly different, not necessarily particular or specialized enough for or like long tail enough potentially for it to perform well on socials.

What we could do also is just keep in mind like if there's anything that seems really honed in on pain point or solution, we can flag that for you as I would imagine that might be more it's higher leverage than something that might be slightly more like educational.
So we can do that.
And then anything that.
Is a little bit more product focused or technical value that maybe if you want to like pull a particular instance out of and consider like linking to the blog as well, ideal.

Or even if it's just arming the sales team who are posting more often just so that they have content to kind of, if they're commenting, directing people onto the site also by just saying like, oh, yes, we have a resource related to XYZ.
And maybe just making it more visible for you and for the sales team.
So they know what resources they have, but aligned on like maybe the majority of the content that's betting at organic isn't necessarily going to be your highest leverage for socials.
But is that kind of how you would think about it?
Pain point, product, technical value, and then anything that maybe is a little bit more like maybe some industry trends, but knowing that will probably be light as well.
So, you going to this?
So, no, I was going to
Sorry, guys.
think my audio.
Can you guys hear me?
Yeah.
Okay.
I can barely hear you guys for some reason.
It's like low.
Can you guys hear Mark okay?
Yeah.
Sometimes my headphones do something weird, so.
Okay.
Wait.
I think everybody's back.
I think we're good.
Okay.
What I was going to say is I agree.
I do think like pain points, like having content around that would be helpful.
And maybe we don't necessarily use the blog post.
We could maybe pull some relevant information from it and we can use it for like a carousel, for instance, and like repurpose it.
And, you know, we're not positioning it as like, hey, here's this really long blog post where you can find a ton of details about X, Y, and Z.
We just kind of use that to pull some ideas for content for social.
So, I think that's probably-
The best way to leverage it.
And I think I got access to Airtable, so I can pop in there and start to see.
Amazing.
Here's I can do.
Sorry, go ahead, Mark.
In Airtable.
Well, so I'm wondering about this.
Here, let me share my screen for a second.
Sure.
You know, if I'm in here reviewing blog posts, Ari, here's a couple things we can do.
One is looking at the type of post, right?
Like, the benefits of partial authorization for merchants, probably not going to be a big hit on social media, but, you know, like, top payment processing trends probably would be.
Yep.
So, when I review one of these articles, I then mark it for publication, it moves out of this list, it goes down into editing.
So, I don't know if actually tagging them in Airtable would be the best, because it's just going to go through this conveyor belt, and it might move out of it before you get it.

If to see it, maybe it would be better if I, like, tagged you in the document, like, I mean, what if I just, like, in a blog post like this, if I was going to review it, if I just went into here and, like, you know, I just did something like that every time that I came across a good article that had been reviewed and was thus able to be used.
Is that helpful?
Yes.
The only thing is, it's depending on how much content there is, and it seems like you guys are cranking through these pretty quickly from what I'm seeing on Slack.
It might get hard for me to track everything.

Is it, is there a way to add, I don't know how the air table is set up, but maybe like a little checkbox that says, like, good for social, so maybe I can filter and then see what, what falls under that category?
Do they stay in Airtable after they're published?
They just go into like a published box.
So she'd be able to see it there.
Okay.
Yeah.

Also, I would imagine that while we're kind of repeating that formula in a higher volume of like X, you know, and it is a little bit more of that tofu where potentially for a reader, doesn't have like enough, not enough of a hook potentially to work as well on socials.

We can also just keep an eye out for the ones where it seems specific enough and there's enough like immediate value or it's topical enough, like it's some kind of hook to something related in the industry where maybe it's a higher value carousel.
And then we won't inundate you with options and we can help kind narrow the field for you also just for the days.

Also feel free to like, you know, drop a note and it sounds like you have a ton to work with, but feel free to also tag us on Slack if you're like, I'm having trouble kind of figuring out which of these is the most, what I should prioritize first.
So we can just, yeah, I think having that, like, just like check off on Airtable will make it easy for me to also look for anything that's like relevant.

There's always conversations happening on social and a lot of times like from RapidSide, we don't really jump into any of it or post any relevant content because we just, you know, we don't have like, we're not going to create content on the fly on something.
It takes a while to get our content out.

So, yeah, it would be nice to see, like, I can do social listening and see what are some trending topics and then also pull the list that you guys have that you've checked off as good for social and then kind of see where there's overlap.
I would also be interested just even like anecdotally what you're seeing perform well and what also like what leads are coming in based off of what content you're seeing.
Like if you're posting a lot about a particular pain point or a particular solution and, excuse me, excuse me, sales is seeing that a bunch of people are coming in through that narrative.
That would also be helpful feedback for us just so that we can understand what is resonating in that like place where it's highly visible to the ICP that we're trying to target.
Yeah, that's a good point.

And we, with Octopost, which is launching in two weeks, it's going to make it easier for us to actually be able to see if somebody read something on the website and then went like on to fill out a contact form or download a paper or anything like that.
So we will have a ton of data that we can look at based on that.
That's so nice.

I remember like for us, we always recommended like a really thorough three phase social selling flow because it's the only way that it really, you know, you can actually leverage what you're doing online or get any kind of great insights from it.
But it was so onerous and like manual.
No one ever did it.
So that's great that there are now tools to.
Yeah, it makes it so, I mean, it wasn't easy to set up, but it was set up.
It's great to see that it's just really pulling a report and it automatically generates like the link trackers for everything.

So if our sales team creates content of their own that we didn't give them, they just like scheduled it in OctoPost because they saw a cool blog on Rapyd, it automatically applies all of the tracking parameters for them and it makes sure that everything is trackable and easy to just pull reports for.
So it's awesome.
Cool.
Any other like anything else super top of mind for you or also like with Stablecoin, maybe that's one too where the more Tofu content might actually be somewhat helpful.
So we can flag that as well.
But yeah, maybe a carousel on like something more educational or neutral could actually be advantageous there.
Do you guys happen to have any handy prompts to transmutate the blogs into other forms of content?
Currently, no, like we're not running socials for really any other.
I don't at the moment, but I could definitely pull together just like a general framework of if it's this, then this would be a simple way to repurpose it.
So yeah, let me think about that and pull something together maybe by the end of the week and share it with you and Ari.
I know you'll, you know, maybe it'll be, maybe it'll be helpful, maybe we won't, but happy to do that.
Yeah.

I was just thinking, Ari, if we identify some of these, like you add a checkbox and I, whenever I'm reviewing blogs, Nicolette's reviewing blogs, whomever, when we see a good one, even, I think on your side, growthx can say, hey, this would be a good one for socials.
It might be cool to have like a, you know, a prompt where you can just grab these because they're very long.
Some of these things are like 11 pages long.
It might be helpful to have something that could maybe like pull out the key points and like spit out a copy for a carousel and a couple of posts for various social media channels.
Obviously you'd still have to refine it, but that could be easier.
We used to do something similar.
I used to be at Verbatim.
And we didn't actually integrate AI at Verbatim while I was there.
But this would be a relatively seamless flow if you just even pull.
And sorry, this probably feels like really basic.
And you're like, no, I already know how to do this.

But I'm pulling it into, yeah, like a cloud project or something and saying like, hey, digest this 11 page piece into something, into a carousel, keeping like each thing at X number of, and then like four slides or whatever it is, and then spinning it up into an infographic, I think is a great way to make it a little bit more digestible to your point, Mark, for socials.
Yeah, definitely.

And I feel like because this is longer form content, it's perfect for carousels and carousels are our top performers, like always 30% plus engagement rate, which like normally engagement is like 8% for a regular post.
So I would love to do more of that.
Something that's top of mind.
So we are.
Obviously have Stablecoin and then Mark and Nicolette, our next conversation after this call is going to be about iGaming content.
So also if there's any iGaming specific content in Airtable that we can flag for social, I think that would also be perfect.
There's actually a lot that's already been published for iGaming too.
Perfect.
At least one 10 iGaming trends you should know is the first one that comes to mind.
It's perfect.
Yeah.
Cool.
I can't filter by iGaming, right?
I have to filter by a high opportunity and then they'll be in that.
So I can do that on the next call.
Okay.
Yeah, that would be helpful because now that for context, Mara, we've been doing social activations to capture these like specific audiences that are at the events that we're attending.
And we just did SBC, which is the event that we all attended in Lisbon.
And from that activation, we got 450 new followers that are in the iGaming space.
So now we're trying to figure out like.
How can we crank out some iGaming specific content on social to kind of capitalize on these new followers?
That's cool.
And that's, I'm assuming that's the company account?
Yes, it's the Rapid LinkedIn account.
Okay, lovely.
Because that's like, I feel like that's also just the only place where you can really, you want to be producing content that's a little bit more neutral, educational.
So it's nice that that's like, you've got that captive audience and now it's just a meeting point.
Cool.
Okay.
Great.
Sounds good.
Anything else that you think like is a high priority for you that we should keep in mind just to be helpful with flagging things for you?

Honestly, I think like the content that I find performs the best is always like top 10 X or like anything that feels like it's like a list of sorts or like how to do X, Y, and Z, or these are the trends we're seeing.
Anything like that like is very easy to create like strong hooks for and have people really engage.
So anything like that, I think, would be helpful.
Okay, cool.
I'll keep doing my listicles, too, when I'm looking through.
Where the heck does the term listicle come from, anyway?
I don't know.
I feel like it was all the rage in media, and like five years ago, it was like everyone was doing it.
Maybe BuzzFeed is the origin.
It's a weird word, and I didn't know if it was like, like blog, I know it like, what, wait, like it's a, I forget what blog actually is a mashup of, it's a port into or something, right?
It's like two.
Yeah, I feel like it's like the, it's the new age version of an article where it's actually just a list, because no one has the attention span, so they just married those two words together.
Oh, article and list.
There it is.
That's what it is.
Listicle.
Listicle.
Yeah.
Okay, anything else that can be helpful?
Back back, so I want to be as helpful as possible, and like, I don't have to run, but also if you've got back to back.
And this was sufficient.
And we've kind of gotten to zero to one.
Happy to let you guys go.
And I'm available whenever if you want to meet up again.

Yeah.

Story that they were in, the topic cluster.

me go to here.

go...

can say like, Hey, I think this would be a particularly strong one, um, for socials to consider.

I'll find it.
Cool.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on social media strategy and content integration between GrowthX and Rapyd](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Rapyd is integrating social media more broadly, moving from siloed operations to a more collaborative approach](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=209.0)
  - [GrowthX's SEO-focused content may not always be ideal for social, but can be repurposed for carousels and sales enablement](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=337.0)
  - [New tool Octopost launching in 2 weeks will improve tracking and data analysis for social selling efforts](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=791.0)
  - [iGaming content is a priority due to recent follower growth from event activation (450 new followers)](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=1068.0)

## Topics

### Current Social Media Approach

  - [Ari pulls content from blog, white papers, and reports for social calendar](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=216.0)
  - [Campaign launches and events drive content, with evergreen content filling gaps](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=240.0)
  - [Moving towards better integration between social and content teams](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=250.0)
  - [Focus on creating content for brand accounts and sales enablement](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)

### Content Collaboration Strategy

  - [GrowthX primarily focuses on SEO and GEO content](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=337.0)
  - [Not all SEO content suitable for social media promotion](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [Will flag content with strong pain points, product focus, or technical value](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=401.0)
  - [Consider repurposing content for carousels (30%+ engagement rate vs. 8% for regular posts)](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=519.0)
  - [Add "good for social" checkbox in Airtable for easy filtering](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=541.0)

### Sales Enablement and Social Selling

  - [Implementing Octopost tool for sales team social media activity](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=272.0)
  - [Tool will provide better tracking and data on content performance](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=791.0)
  - [Automatically applies tracking parameters to all shared content](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=847.0)

### iGaming Content Focus

  - [Recent event (SBC in Lisbon) brought 450 new iGaming followers to Rapyd LinkedIn](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=1073.0)
  - [Need to capitalize on new audience with targeted iGaming content](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
  - [Will identify and flag existing iGaming content for social media use](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=1316.0)

### Content Performance Insights

  - [Top-performing content includes:](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=1120.0)
      - ["Top 10" lists](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=1120.0)
      - ["How-to" guides](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=1120.0)
      - [Trend analysis pieces](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=1120.0)
  - [Listicles and easily digestible formats work well for engagement](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=1120.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX to flag high-potential social content in Airtable](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=401.0)
  - [Mark to pull iGaming content for Ari's review](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=1316.0)
  - [Mara to share published and pipeline iGaming content via Slack](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=1358.0)
  - [Consider developing AI prompts to repurpose long-form content into social-friendly formats](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
  - [Continue focus on creating list-based and trend-focused content for social media](https://fathom.video/share/Yzdx9ztamFRaDBPuyDKCuyJQdi2gRAga?tab=summary&timestamp=1120.0)

action_items.description: Add 'Good for Social' checkbox column in Airtable for content team to flag suitable posts
Create/share framework for repurposing blog content into social posts (carousels, etc.) by end of week
Compile list of published + pipeline iGaming content, share in Slack, tag Ari
